creator: matt@tnpi.net
text:
# pkg install vim
New packages to be INSTALLED:
	libffi: 3.4.4_1
	mpdecimal: 2.5.1
	python39: 3.9.18_1
	vim: 9.1.0158
	xxd: 9.1.0158

The process will require 161 MiB more space.

161 MiB just for vim?

What I want and expect is the console flavor of vim with syntax files and the basic goodies that make vim better than vi. Colors. Syntax highlighting. Those niceties are stripped from vim-tiny. What I do NOT want or expect is bundling 100+ MiB of language interpreters just to get vim. By all means, have packages pre-built for popular options, but that shouldn't be the default. Users wanting a bundled language interpreter should expect to specify a flavor, such as vim-python, vim-perl, or vim-lua.
